name: Huesca city - circular running from University
name_original: Huesca ciudad – circular de running desde Universidad
region_intro: Jogging around Hoya De Huesca offers diverse landscapes, from the Pre-Pyrenean mountains to the Ebro valley. The region features varied terrain, including impressive rocky outcrops known as Mallos, river courses, and agricultural plains. Runners can find routes winding through valleys, alongside rivers, and around reservoirs, providing a range of natural features for exploration. This area is characterized by its geological formations and extensive network of trails suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Hoya De Huesca The most popular jogging route is Trail of the Sasos of Tierz and Bellestar, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the local agricultural landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop, a difficult 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This route provides views of the iconic Mallos de Riglos rock formations and includes significant elevation gain. Local runners also love the Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through the scenic area around the Embalse de Arguis reservoir, often completed in about 43 minutes. Jogging around Hoya De Huesca is defined by Pre-Pyrenean mountains, the striking Mallos rock formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging trail runs with elevation changes. The routes in Hoya De Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Hoya De Huesca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Hoya de Huesca offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 250 trails available. These range from easy paths to challenging trail runs with significant elevation changes, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The region boasts a spectacular variety of terrains. You'll find routes winding through Pre-Pyrenean mountains and picturesque valleys, alongside rivers like the Gállego and Flumen, and around reservoirs such as the Embalse de Arguis. The landscape also features impressive rocky outcrops known as the Mallos, agricultural plains, and areas with holm oaks, pines, and Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in the area?
A: Yes, Hoya de Huesca has routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949583597/huesca-city-circular-running-from-university" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca city - circular running from University&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7 km (2.9 miles) route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed run. There are 11 easy routes in total.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Hoya de Huesca are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950281532/embalse-de-arguis-circular-trail-running-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949583597/huesca-city-circular-running-from-university" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesca city - circular running from University&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: You can encounter several striking natural features. The iconic &lt;strong&gt;Mallos de Riglos&lt;/strong&gt; are impressive rocky outcrops visible from routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950292551/mallos-de-riglos-path-of-heaven-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadiello Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Escalete&lt;/a&gt; gorge are also significant. For historical sights, the impressive &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Loarre_Castle/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent Romanesque landmark in the region.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Yes, many trails provide stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950292551/mallos-de-riglos-path-of-heaven-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking views of the Mallos. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cielo - Panoramic View&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4630341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colorado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are specific highlights known for their scenic outlooks.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The running routes in Hoya de Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic Mallos to serene river valleys, and the extensive network of trails that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Hoya de Huesca?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the easier and moderate routes with less elevation gain are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949560345/trail-of-the-sasos-of-tierz-and-bellestar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Sasos of Tierz and Bellestar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.2 km (4.5 miles) route through agricultural landscapes that could be enjoyed by active families.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The region's climate makes it suitable for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, especially in the plains, so early mornings or late evenings are preferable. Winters are milder than in the high Pyrenees, but higher elevation routes might experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for more experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Hoya de Huesca offers several longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20155998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Romanesque Church of Arguis loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 29 km (18 miles) trail with significant elevation changes, ideal for experienced trail runners.

Q: Are there any routes near the Embalse de Arguis reservoir?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Arguis is a popular spot for runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950281532/embalse-de-arguis-circular-trail-running-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arguis – circular trail running route&lt;/a&gt; is a scenic 5.4 km (3.4 miles) loop. For a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14942420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir – Arguis Reservoir Dam loop from Arguis&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 14 km (8.7 miles) with considerable elevation.

Q: Can I run along rivers in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Yes, several rivers flow through the region, often accompanied by trails. The Flumen river, for instance, is featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Flumen Trail loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, offering a pleasant 7.4 km (4.6 miles) run along its banks.
